title: Bidens atrosanguinea
taxonomy: Bidens atrosanguinea is classified within the family Asteraceae. This family comprises flowering plants commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. The genus Bidens includes around 220 species of plants, many of which are annuals or perennials with small to medium-sized flowers. Bidens atrosanguinea, as part of the Bidens genus, is native to tropical Africa. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Bidens atrosanguinea is closely related to other plants such as dandelions and zinnias.
uses: Bidens atrosanguinea, also known as the red-stemmed beggar-ticks, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and stems are used in herbal remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Bidens atrosanguinea is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In some traditional medicine practices, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Bidens atrosanguinea for medicinal purposes.

intro: Bidens atrosanguinea, commonly known as red-stemmed beggarticks, is a flowering plant species native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various health benefits, including treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
